title: Self-Esteem & Self-Hatred
lead: The way you talk to yourself shapes your whole inner world. A harsh inner critic is learned — which means a kinder one can be learned too.
explanation: Self-esteem is how you value and regard yourself. When it's low, you may feel not good enough, unlovable, or like a fraud — and treat yourself with a harshness you'd never aim at someone you love.

Self-hatred usually isn't the truth about you; it's an old, internalised voice — often absorbed from criticism, comparison, trauma, or hard experiences. It can feel like fact, but it's a pattern of thought, and patterns can change.

The goal isn't arrogance or forced positivity. It's self-compassion: treating yourself as a flawed, worthy human being, the way you would a good friend. Research shows self-compassion is a stronger foundation for wellbeing than self-esteem built on being 'better than' others.
symptoms: Feeling not good enough or unlovable

immediate.title: The friend test
steps: Notice the harsh thing you're telling yourself
Ask: would I say this to a friend I love who was struggling?
If not, offer yourself the words you'd give them instead

immediate.title: Self-compassion break
steps: Acknowledge: 'This is a moment of suffering'
Remember: 'Suffering is part of being human — I'm not alone'
Place a hand on your heart and offer yourself kindness: 'May I be gentle with myself'

immediate.title: Separate thought from truth
immediate.description: When you notice 'I'm worthless', try adding: 'I'm having the thought that I'm worthless.' A thought is a mental event, not a verdict on your worth.

longTerm.title: Practise self-compassion
longTerm.description: Self-compassion is a skill with strong research support. Regular practice measurably reduces self-criticism and builds resilience.

longTerm.title: Challenge the inner critic (CBT)
longTerm.description: CBT helps you catch, question, and rebalance the automatic critical thoughts that fuel low self-worth.

longTerm.title: Build worth through values, not comparison
longTerm.description: Acting in line with what matters to you — kindness, honesty, effort — builds a stable sense of worth that doesn't depend on being better than others.

resources.title: Self-compassion exercises & practices
resources.whyHere: Free guided practices from the leading researcher on self-compassion.

resources.title: Building self-esteem self-help guide
resources.whyHere: Free CBT-based booklet for improving self-esteem.

resources.title: The space between self-esteem and self-compassion
resources.whyHere: Clear talk on why self-compassion is a healthier foundation than self-esteem.

resources.title: Healthy Minds Program
resources.whyHere: Free training that includes cultivating a kinder relationship with yourself.
